# Thread title is generated by a one-shot non-Run worker at creation

A Thread's title is **generated by a short LLM call at creation**, from the
user's first prompt, by a **bare one-shot Worker** — a second `ChildWorker`
subprocess spawned fire-and-forget from the `thread/create` handler, **not a
Run**. It mints no `runs` row, no assistant Message, no hub, and nothing in run
history. The truncated-prompt title that `thread/create` writes synchronously
(the prior behavior) becomes a **placeholder**: it is shown immediately, and the
generated title overwrites it in `threads.title` when (and only when) generation
succeeds.

This **supersedes** the `thread/create` behavior described in
[ADR-0022](./0022-run-event-delivery-hub-snapshot-tail.md) and implemented in
`runs/thread_create.rs`, where the title was *defined as* the prompt truncated
to 80 scalars. That truncation is retained, but only as the placeholder. *(The
placeholder derivation is later refined by
[ADR-0048](./0048-thread-title-fallback-slug.md): a word-boundary slug trimmed
to ~32 scalars replaces the 80-scalar mid-word truncation. The **generated**
title's `TITLE_MAX_CHARS` cap below is unaffected.)*

## Context

Until now a Thread's sidebar label was the first message verbatim, cut to 80
characters. It is a poor label: it repeats the prompt's opening words rather
than naming what the thread is *about*, and a long first sentence is truncated
mid-thought.

Core cannot call an LLM in-process: it owns no provider client, no model
catalog at the call site, no OAuth token plumbing. Its **only** path to a model
is to spawn a Worker subprocess (`packages/worker`) over the NDJSON Tool
Protocol (ADR-0013/0018/0026). The Worker interpreter is Workflow-agnostic — fed
a manifest with a system prompt, a user prompt, and an empty tool set, it streams
the model's reply as `text_delta`s then `done`. So a title generator is just a
**second, concurrent Worker spawn with a different manifest** — reusing the
entire provider/model/auth/transport stack rather than reimplementing it in Rust.

The decision is *what kind of* second spawn. A title is not a turn in the
conversation: it must not persist an assistant Message, must not occupy run
history, must not publish Run Events, and must not be resumable. Making it a Run
would force all of that machinery to exist and then be hidden. So the titler is
a **bare one-shot**: it reuses the `ChildWorker` *transport* directly, driven by
a tiny collector loop — not the `run_loop` — that accumulates `text_delta`s and
stops on `done`/`error`/EOF.

## Decision

- **Trigger & context.** Generated once, at `thread/create`, from the **prompt
  only** (not the reply — there is no reply yet). No re-titling as the thread
  grows.

- **Not a Run.** A bare one-shot `ChildWorker` spawned `tokio::spawn`
  fire-and-forget *after* `persist_thread_with_first_run` commits (so the create
  response is never blocked and the placeholder already exists). A throwaway
  `Uuid` is minted for the manifest's `run_id`, used **only** for ADR-0038 log
  correlation; nothing touches the `runs` table.

- **Own launch role.** A new `launch::Role::Titler` with its own
  `INKSTONE_TITLE_WORKER_CMD` override (and the same `cli.ts` tsx default /
  `inkstone-worker` sibling-binary default as `Role::Worker`). The title-ness
  lives in the manifest, not a different program; the separate role keeps the
  titler independently injectable in tests and decoupled from the run Worker's
  command.

- **Bespoke minimal manifest.** Built directly — **not** via the dispatcher,
  the Workflow catalog, settings, or `skills::augmented_system_prompt`. A
  hardcoded title-generation system prompt, `tools: []` (so no ambient
  `load_skill` — `run_descriptors` is bypassed), `thinking_level: "off"` (the
  interpreter already omits the reasoning param for `"off"`), empty history,
  `prompt` = the user's first message. It reuses the existing `WorkerManifest` /
  `WorkflowManifest` wire types verbatim — **no protocol/parity change**.

- **Model.** A fixed small model, not user-configurable:
  `default_title_model("openai-codex") = "gpt-5.4-mini"`, resolved as
  `default_title_model(provider).filter(is_known_model).or_else(|| default_model(provider))`
  so an absent/unknown title model falls back to the chat default. Provider is
  the default Workflow's provider.

- **Connected-only spawn.** The provider access token is resolved *before*
  building the manifest. The titler spawns **only** on `Ok(Some(token))` — an
  unconnected provider (`Ok(None)`) or a resolution error both skip the spawn
  and keep the placeholder, with zero wasted subprocess. (This is deliberately
  stricter than the run Worker, which proceeds tokenless so the provider call
  surfaces an auth error to the user; a titler has no user-facing failure
  channel, so a tokenless attempt would only burn a subprocess.)

- **Time-bounded.** The collector is wrapped in
  `tokio::time::timeout(INKSTONE_TITLE_TIMEOUT_MS, default 15_000ms)`. On elapse
  the Worker is shut down and dropped (`kill_on_drop` reaps the child) and the
  placeholder stays. A detached fire-and-forget task with no hub and no
  user-facing cancel path would otherwise leak the task + child on a wedged
  model.

- **Free-text output, sanitized, always used.** The accumulated text is cleaned
  in code (the model output is free text — the wire carries no structured
  channel): strip `<think>…</think>` and orphan tags, take the first non-empty
  line, collapse internal whitespace, strip one layer of wrapping quotes, and
  truncate to `TITLE_MAX_CHARS` (80) on a scalar boundary. A non-empty result
  overwrites the title via `db::update_thread_title`; an **empty-after-sanitize**
  result keeps the placeholder. Suspicious-but-non-empty output is **not**
  rejected (no surveyed peer tool does, and it risks discarding valid titles).

- **Delivery is lazy.** The generated title lands in `threads.title`; clients
  pick it up on the next `thread/list` read (a cold load / reconnect). There is
  **no** live push to an open client, and **no** retry on failure — both are
  deferred (see Related: follow-up issue). *(Live push superseded by
  [ADR-0047](./0047-connection-notification-channel.md): the title is now pushed
  to the creating connection via the run-less notification channel; lazy
  `thread/list` remains the fallback when the push can't be delivered. Retry
  stays deferred.)*

## Considered and rejected

- **Make the titler a real Run** (reuse `spawn`/`run_loop`). Rejected: it mints a
  `runs` row + assistant Message and surfaces a phantom turn in history that then
  has to be hidden everywhere; the "reuse" saving is illusory once the hiding is
  counted. The bare one-shot reuses the transport without the Run semantics.

- **Generate after the first full turn** (prompt + reply), as Continue/Gemini
  do — a richer signal. Rejected for v1: it couples titling to run completion and
  delays the title; prompt-only is what the majority of surveyed tools
  (opencode, Goose, Crush) do and is the cheapest correct thing. Revisit if
  one-line prompts yield thin titles.

- **Block `thread/create` on the title.** Rejected: it defeats pure-subscribe's
  instant thread-open (ADR-0022) for a whole extra LLM round-trip.

- **Structured output** (a forced `set_title` tool, or a new wire frame).
  Rejected: zero precedent across seven surveyed peer tools (all free-text), and
  it buys schema-validation a single short string does not need while adding a
  manifest/protocol path. Free text + sanitize is unanimous practice.

- **A second `title` Workflow in the catalog.** Rejected: it would route through
  the shared manifest builder (injecting `<available_skills>` + ambient
  `load_skill`, which a titler must not see) and add a product "Workflow" that
  isn't one. The bespoke builder keeps the titler out of the catalog, settings,
  and dispatcher.

## Related

- [ADR-0022](./0022-run-event-delivery-hub-snapshot-tail.md) — `thread/create`
  message-first creation; this ADR supersedes its title-from-prompt definition
  (truncation → placeholder).
- [ADR-0023](./0023-provider-oauth-core-owned-credentials.md) — Core-owned
  credentials; the titler's connected-only gate reads the same store.
- [ADR-0024](./0024-user-configurable-model-and-effort.md) — model/effort resolution;
  `default_title_model` is product policy authored beside `default_model`.
- [ADR-0041](./0041-compiled-worker-binaries.md) — the role-keyed launch
  resolver `Role::Titler` extends.
- [ADR-0047](./0047-connection-notification-channel.md) — the run-less
  connection-notification channel that delivers the generated title live to the
  creating connection, superseding this ADR's lazy-only delivery.
- [ADR-0048](./0048-thread-title-fallback-slug.md) — supersedes this ADR's
  placeholder definition: the create-time fallback is a word-boundary slug
  (~32 scalars), not the prompt truncated to 80 scalars; the generated title's
  `TITLE_MAX_CHARS` cap is retained.
- Follow-up issue: transient-failure retry affordance, still out of scope (live
  delivery shipped in ADR-0047).
